title: Premium (Customer Incentive) Liability
event: Hollis Co. sold 400,000 cereal boxes this year. Each premium (a bowl costing $1.50) requires 10 boxtops, and 60% of boxtops are expected to be redeemed. By year-end, 150,000 boxtops have been redeemed. Compute premium expense and the year-end premium liability.

computationPaths.narration: Expense the WHOLE expected cost in the sales year (matching); the liability is what's expected but not yet redeemed.

memorize.body: Expense = total EXPECTED redemptions × unit cost, in the year of sale. Liability = expected − already redeemed.
Same architecture as warranties and bad debts: estimate with the sale, consume with the claims.

questions.prompt: Expected premium cost $36,000; premiums redeemed to date cost $22,500. Year-end premium liability = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the full expected cost — 22,500 of it has already been satisfied. The remaining obligation is 13,500.
That's what's been REDEEMED. The liability is the expected redemptions still outstanding: 13,500.
